summary: Frontend-only testing of new /help Help & Guide page. Verified role-aware content, sidebar nav visibility, quick-nav cards, search/filter, accordion expand, empty state, and route accessibility for super_admin (admin@test.com), resource (riley@test.com), and client (client@test.com). All 9 requested test cases pass. One minor DOM nesting warning found (Badge div inside p).

ui_bugs.issue:
React console warning: '

cannot contain a nested

' / 'validateDOMNesting'. The component (rendered as a
) is placed inside a

. This produces a hydration warning on every page load and every role login (super_admin, resource, client all show it). Fix: wrap the paragraph text in a or change the outer

to a

.

action_items:
Fix DOM nesting warning in /app/frontend/src/pages/Help.js (~line 490): the Badge (div) is nested inside a

. Change the wrapping

to a

or wrap surrounding text in so the Badge is not a descendant of a paragraph.
critical_code_review_comments: Help.js is 616 lines — still within acceptable size, but SECTIONS content array (~330 lines) could be extracted to /app/frontend/src/data/helpContent.js for cleanliness and easier localization.
RichText uses dangerouslySetInnerHTML with manual replacements. Since content is hard-coded internally this is acceptable, but if content ever becomes user-generated it would be a XSS risk.
Nav item includes role 'client', so clients get 'Help & Guide' in the sidebar even though the Layout normally suppresses most items for clients. This is intentional per the spec and works correctly.
context_for_next_testing_agent:
Help page is fully functional. Route /help works. Role-aware filtering, search, accordions, quick-nav cards, and empty state all work. The only issue is a React DOM nesting warning caused by Badge (div) inside

in Help.js header (line ~490). Not a blocker.
